quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste hatred moved into the board, driven by a claim that a baniyapoo initiated all caste wars. The system brought a flurry of replies and counter-replies, with Anon fueling the storm.

A scattered high-pressure ridge of intellectual curiosity formed over the board, as Anon wondered how to attract high IQ users to Indiachan. The discussion drew a few scattered showers of suggestions, but no clear front of consensus emerged.

A fog of speculation rolled in around elite baby-eating conspiracies, with Anon comparing cultural narratives between India and the West.

Meanwhile, a drizzle of personal anecdotes and complaints touched down in the notices section, with Anon sharing stories of a BSNL interview fiasco and musings on SREE RANDI.

articles.title: Anon Claims Baniyapoo Initiated Caste Wars
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion, stating 'It was a baniyapoo who initiated all the caste wars.' Replies to the OP largely mocked and insulted various castes, with one anon saying 'Niggerwals originate from chadyana but they are even high aasi than literal chudas and valmiks.' Another anon described Niggerwals as 'pure street shitters' and 'subhumans.' Anon also joked about the perceived social status of different castes, with one saying 'Kek trvth nuke AASI max baniyapoo They are so ugly that their jeetas are immune to getting r*ped.' Some anons engaged in direct insults, with one telling another to 'Go back to shitter.' Anon also discussed reservations, with one saying 'I think everyone who does not take any kind of reservation is UC.

Everyone else is pretty much bhangi to me.' Anon continued to exchange insults and jokes about various castes, including Baniyas, Chamars, and Niggerwals.

Anon questioned the intelligence and behavior of various castes, with one saying 'They are ugly for sure but at least they are intelligent and well behaved, far better than you chamars.' The discussion also touched on the topic of who might be considered 'UC' (likely 'Upper Caste') and who might not be, with one anon stating 'Kek baniypoos are so disgusting and AASImaxxx that even pajeeria gobermint won't give them reservation.'

articles.title: Attracting High IQ Users to Indiachan
articles.body:
Anon kicked off a discussion on how to attract high IQ users to Indiachan. The original poster asked, 'I want inch to have high iq users.

How to attract high iq users on inch?' One respondent suggested that Indiachan should not be a 'horde spawn point,' implying that it should avoid becoming a platform for large numbers of low-quality posters.

Another anon simply stated that Indiachan's users are better than those on Reddit and Instagram.

A third poster poked fun at the idea of high IQ users on Indiachan, saying 'Inch, High IQ, IQ, Top kek.' This response seemed to express skepticism about the possibility of attracting high IQ users to the platform.

A brief exchange followed, with one user responding to a comment with 'Rota reh,' which seemed to be a dismissive or uninterested remark.

The conversation remained lighthearted and joking, with no clear solution offered for attracting high IQ users to Indiachan.

The discussion did not provide a concrete answer to the original poster's question, but it did offer some insight into the community's dynamics and attitudes towards intelligence and user quality.

articles.title: Elite Baby-Eating Conspiracies: A Cultural Comparison
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion by questioning why India doesn't have conspiracy theories about elites eating babies, contrasting it with the prevalence of such theories in Western countries.

Another anon chimed in with a response that seemed to imply a connection between the socioeconomic and intellectual profiles of those creating conspiracy theories and the communities they belong to.

They mentioned "high iq white autists making conspiracy theories about jews vs slumdwelling chamars making stories about evils of pongas." One respondent offered a more straightforward explanation, referencing an earlier post: "Indian elites don't eat babies because of protein." This comment seemed to suggest that the reason for the absence of such conspiracy theories in India might be related to dietary habits or cultural perceptions of elite behavior.

A different anon provided a concise answer: "The answer is always caste." This response implied that the caste system might play a significant role in shaping narratives and beliefs within Indian society, potentially influencing the types of conspiracy theories that emerge.

articles.title: BSNL interview fiasco
articles.body:
Anon woke up to a call from a HR person after their profile was viewed on Naukri. The HR asked them to show up for an interview on short notice, but Anon requested to reschedule for the next day.

Anon cited their current location and a shaky BSNL connection as reasons for the delay. The HR person instead asked them to send their resume for further consideration.

Another anon chimed in, calling the situation 'brootal' and saying 'nobody cares'.

articles.title: Anon muses on SREE RANDI
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with 'SREE RANDI
JAI JAI SHERANDI'. One anon asked how Muslims supposedly attract Hindu women easily, citing 'some charm to their personality' lacking in Hindus.

Another anon brought up a Twitter personality, describing them as a randi who was attracted to 'paki chads'. A respondent joked that not caring about treating women poorly can make one sexy.

Anon also reacted to an unseen profile, saying they couldn't find it to 'muth maarne ke liye'.
